items.fix: Pass raw logits to nn.CrossEntropyLoss; the loss applies log-softmax internally. Use NLLLoss only if you already computed log-probabilities.
items.why: Double softmax or wrong input shape produces incorrect gradients and meaningless loss values.
items.example: Wrong: loss_fn(model(x), y) where model ends with softmax. Right: linear logits then CrossEntropyLoss(logits, y).

items.fix: Always treat P as the true/reference distribution and Q as the model: H(P, Q) = −Σ p log q. In classification, P is (one-hot) labels, Q is predictions.
items.why: Swapping roles changes the formula and breaks the link to KL divergence D_KL(P || Q).
items.example: For true class index 2, only q(2) enters −log q(2); p is 1 on class 2 and 0 elsewhere.

items.mistake: Using unnormalized scores as q(x) in manual loss code
items.fix: Normalize Q to sum to 1 (softmax) before taking −log q(y), or use log_softmax for numerical stability.
items.why: Cross-entropy requires a valid PMF; arbitrary scores are not probabilities and can yield negative or undefined loss.
items.example: Logits [10, 10, 10] give softmax [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]; loss = −log(1/3) ≈ 1.1 nats, not −log(10).
